gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 initially found that legacy main did not honor a monkeypatched legacy release_bundle_to_record on the output-json path and that the legacy star-import surface lost RELEASE_BUNDLE_PACKAGE_NAME. I added a facade-local legacy writer, restored the package-name constant in the legacy facade, added regressions for both behaviors, and follow-up review approved with no remaining blockers.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
repository secret-pattern scan across docs, source, tests, databricks, pr-evidence, and GitHub metadata
PYTHONPATH=src python import-boundary and legacy writer/star-import probes
GPT-5.5 follow-up review after compatibility fixes
what_changed: Moved release bundle packaging implementation into document_kv_cache.release_bundle with a curated document __all__ surface.
Replaced restaurant_kv_serving.release_bundle with a compatibility facade that preserves the broad legacy star-import surface and owns a local main function.
Added regressions for document and legacy CLI hook behavior, legacy output-json serialization hooks, legacy star-import compatibility, public module ownership, and explicit legacy-test governance.
why: Release bundles are part of the open-source package release workflow, so implementation ownership should live in the document package while legacy restaurant imports continue to work during migration.
